channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Kofi Annan NextGen Democracy Prize 2026: $10,000 Award, Global Recognition, and One Young World Summit Scholarship for Young Democracy Leaders
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Kofi Annan NextGen Democracy Prize 2026 is a prestigious global award designed to recognize and empower outstanding young leaders who are actively defending and advancing democracy in their communities and across the world. Launched by the Kofi Annan Foundation in partnership with the Democracy and Culture Foundation, this prize celebrates the legacy of Kofi […]

The post Kofi Annan NextGen Democracy Prize 2026: $10,000 Award, Global Recognition, and One Young World Summit Scholarship for Young Democracy Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Kofi Annan NextGen Democracy Prize 2026 is a prestigious global award designed to recognize and empower outstanding young leaders who are actively defending and advancing democracy in their communities and across the world.

Launched by the Kofi Annan Foundation in partnership with the Democracy and Culture Foundation, this prize celebrates the legacy of Kofi Annan by identifying courageous young changemakers who are driving democratic progress, fostering inclusive governance, and strengthening civic participation globally.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the Kofi Annan NextGen Democracy Prize 2026 is:

17 May 2026 (23:59 BST)

Applications must be submitted in full before the deadline, including all required supporting materials.

About the Kofi Annan NextGen Democracy Prize

The prize was established in 2023 to:

  • Recognize exceptional young leaders promoting democracy
  • Inspire youth engagement in governance and civic participation
  • Strengthen democratic systems through youth-led innovation

Named after former UN Secretary-General Kofi Annan, the initiative reflects his belief that democracy is essential for better governance, peace, and human development.

At a time when democracy is facing significant global challenges—including rising authoritarianism, conflict, and weakened institutions—this prize seeks to spotlight young leaders who are taking bold action to protect democratic values.

What the Prize Offers

The Kofi Annan NextGen Democracy Prize provides both financial support and global exposure to selected candidates.

Key Benefits

  1. Financial Award
    • The winner receives USD 10,000
  2. Mentorship Opportunity
    • One-year mentorship with a recognized democracy expert
  3. Global Recognition
    • Award presented at the prestigious Athens Democracy Forum
  4. Fully Funded Summit Participation
    • Winner and nine finalists receive a scholarship to attend the One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town, South Africa (3–6 November 2026)
  5. International Platform
    • Opportunity to speak at a high-level global forum
    • Increased visibility and credibility for your work

Focus Areas: What Kind of Work is Recognized?

Applicants are evaluated based on their contributions to advancing democracy. The prize highlights leaders who have taken meaningful actions such as:

  • Promoting inclusive participation in decision-making processes
  • Encouraging peaceful dialogue on political issues
  • Supporting intergenerational engagement in governance
  • Amplifying marginalized voices and communities
  • Advocating for free and fair elections
  • Protecting fundamental freedoms such as press, speech, and assembly
  • Strengthening democratic institutions
  • Driving reforms to sustain and modernize democratic systems

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for the Kofi Annan NextGen Democracy Prize 2026, applicants must:

  1. Age Requirement
    • Be between 18 and 30 years old
  2. Leadership Experience
    • Demonstrate proven leadership in promoting democracy
  3. Availability
    • Be willing and able to participate in:
      • Athens Democracy Forum
      • One Young World Summit 2026
  4. Language Requirement
    • Submit the application in English

  5. Required Submissions
    Applicants must provide:

    • A short video explaining:
      • Their work in defending democracy
      • Their motivation
    • A letter of support from a recognized organization or expert in democracy, which must include:
      • Full name, organization, and title of the referee
      • Relationship with the applicant
      • Explanation of the applicant’s impact on democracy

    Important note:

    • The referee must not be a family member, friend, or direct colleague
    • Consent must be obtained before submitting their details

Selection and Evaluation Criteria

Applications are assessed based on:

  • Demonstrated leadership and initiative
  • Measurable impact in promoting democracy
  • Innovation and effectiveness of actions
  • Commitment to democratic values
  • Potential for future influence and scalability

The selection process aims to identify individuals who are not only impactful but also capable of inspiring broader societal change.

Key Dates and Timeline

  • Applications Open: 15 April 2026
  • Application Deadline: 17 May 2026
  • Selection Process: Mid-2026
  • One Young World Summit: 3–6 November 2026
  • Award Ceremony: Athens Democracy Forum (2026)

Why You Should Apply

The Kofi Annan NextGen Democracy Prize is more than an award—it is a global platform for young leaders to amplify their voice and scale their impact.

Key Reasons to Apply

  • Gain international recognition for your work
  • Receive funding and mentorship
  • Join a global network of changemakers
  • Access high-level platforms and decision-makers
  • Inspire others to engage in democracy

This opportunity is ideal for passionate young leaders committed to shaping inclusive, accountable, and resilient democratic systems worldwide.

How to Apply

Applicants must:

  1. Complete the online application form
  2. Submit all required documents (video + letter of support)
  3. Ensure compliance with eligibility criteria
  4. Submit before the deadline

APPLY HERE

Click HERE to learn more about the Kofi Annan NextGen Democracy Prize 2026.

Discover more global Award opportunities on Opportunities for Youth HERE.

The post Kofi Annan NextGen Democracy Prize 2026: $10,000 Award, Global Recognition, and One Young World Summit Scholarship for Young Democracy Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: African Leadership Centre (ALC) Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Peace, Security & Development Programs for African and Global South Leaders
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The African Leadership Centre (ALC) Fellowships 2026 present a transformative opportunity for emerging leaders, scholars, and practitioners committed to advancing peace, security, and development across Africa and the Global South. These prestigious fellowships combine academic excellence, policy engagement, mentorship, and real-world experience to equip participants with the tools, networks, and knowledge needed to influence governance, […]

The post African Leadership Centre (ALC) Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Peace, Security & Development Programs for African and Global South Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Leadership Centre (ALC) Fellowships 2026 present a transformative opportunity for emerging leaders, scholars, and practitioners committed to advancing peace, security, and development across Africa and the Global South.

These prestigious fellowships combine academic excellence, policy engagement, mentorship, and real-world experience to equip participants with the tools, networks, and knowledge needed to influence governance, leadership, and development agendas globally.

Application Deadline

All ALC Fellowship programmes currently open for applications share the same deadline:

24 May 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to begin early and carefully prepare all required documents.

About the African Leadership Centre (ALC)

The African Leadership Centre is a globally recognized institution dedicated to:

  • Promoting African-led solutions to global challenges
  • Advancing leadership in peace, security, and development
  • Bridging academic research with policy and practice
  • Building a network of influential scholars and practitioners

Through its fellowship programmes, ALC empowers participants to transform ideas into impactful policies and actions.

Overview of ALC Fellowship Programmes

The ALC offers multiple fellowship tracks tailored to different backgrounds and career stages. Each programme integrates academic study, mentorship, and practical engagement.

1. Fellowships for African Scholars in Peace, Security and Development (Degree-Awarding)

Programme Highlights

  • Duration: 18 months
  • Combines academic study and practical experience
  • Includes a Master’s programme in Global Leadership and Peacebuilding at King’s College London

Key Components

  • Rigorous academic research and policy analysis
  • Impactful writing and leadership training
  • Mentorship (group, one-on-one, peer-to-peer)
  • Simulation exercises and institutional visits
  • Six-month attachment at:
    • African universities
    • Regional organizations
    • Centres of Excellence

Outcome

Fellows graduate with:

  • Advanced academic knowledge
  • Practical policy experience
  • Strong professional networks
  • Leadership capacity to influence Africa’s development agenda

Apply Here

2. Associate Fellowship in Global Leadership and Peacebuilding (Global South)

Who It’s For

  • Students from the Global South
  • Applicants pursuing MSc Global Leadership and Peacebuilding

Benefits

  • 25% tuition fee coverage at King’s College London
  • £5,000 contribution toward living expenses

Programme Features

  • Academic research and policy engagement
  • Mentorship and leadership development
  • Optional internship at international institutions

Additional Requirement

Applicants must also apply separately to King’s College London.

Apply Here

3. Associate Fellowship in Peace, Security and Development (African Students)

Target Group

  • African students pursuing careers in leadership and development

Benefits

  • 25% tuition fee bursary
  • £5,000 living allowance

Unique Advantage

  • Paid six-month attachment at:
    • African universities
    • Regional organizations
    • Centres of Excellence

Programme Outcomes

  • Academic excellence
  • Policy and research expertise
  • Practical leadership experience

Apply Here

4. Peace and Security Fellowship for Sudanese Women (Non-Degree)

Target Group

  • Female citizens of Sudan

Key Requirements

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree
  • Valid travel documents

This fellowship is specifically designed to empower Sudanese women in peace and security leadership roles.

Apply Here

What Makes ALC Fellowships Unique?

1. Academic and Policy Integration

Participants engage in both academic learning and real-world policy work.

2. Mentorship and Leadership Development

  • Group mentoring sessions
  • One-on-one coaching
  • Peer learning

3. Global Networking

  • Connect with scholars and policymakers worldwide
  • Join a network spanning over 30 countries

4. Practical Impact

  • Fellows contribute to evidence-based policy solutions
  • Influence governance and development strategies

Application Process

The ALC Fellowship application process is structured and comprehensive:

Step 1: Select a Programme

Choose the fellowship that aligns with your goals and background.

Step 2: Create an Account

Register on the ALC application portal.

Step 3: Complete Application Form

Provide:

  • Personal information
  • Academic background
  • Professional experience
  • Research proposal

Step 4: Upload Documents

Required documents include:

  • CV
  • Supporting statement
  • Degree certificates
  • Additional materials (PDF, max 10MB each)

Step 5: Nominate Referees

  • Provide details of two referees
  • Referees submit recommendations directly

Step 6: Submit Application

  • Review all entries
  • Submit and receive confirmation

Why You Should Apply

The ALC Fellowships are among the most impactful leadership programmes for individuals passionate about peace, security, and development.

Key Reasons to Apply

  • Gain world-class education and mentorship
  • Access funding and financial support
  • Build a strong international network
  • Acquire practical policy experience
  • Influence real-world governance and development

Click HERE to learn more about the African Leadership Centre (ALC) Fellowships 2026.

For more global Fellowship opportunities, visit Opportunities For Youth HERE.

The post African Leadership Centre (ALC) Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Peace, Security & Development Programs for African and Global South Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Heroes of Tomorrow: UN SDG Action Awards 2026 – Global Recognition & Impact Platform for Changemakers Driving Sustainable Development
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Heroes of Tomorrow: UN SDG Action Awards 2026 is a prestigious global initiative that recognizes and amplifies individuals and initiatives making extraordinary contributions toward achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Organized by the UN SDG Action Campaign, this award program celebrates bold, innovative, and resilient changemakers who are actively shaping a more just, inclusive, […]

The post Heroes of Tomorrow: UN SDG Action Awards 2026 – Global Recognition & Impact Platform for Changemakers Driving Sustainable Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Heroes of Tomorrow: UN SDG Action Awards 2026 is a prestigious global initiative that recognizes and amplifies individuals and initiatives making extraordinary contributions toward achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

Organized by the UN SDG Action Campaign, this award program celebrates bold, innovative, and resilient changemakers who are actively shaping a more just, inclusive, and sustainable world. From grassroots activists to creative innovators, the awards spotlight those whose work is transforming communities and inspiring global action.

Application Deadline

All applications and nominations must be submitted by:

May 17, 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early to ensure a complete and compelling submission.

About the Heroes of Tomorrow: UN SDG Action Awards

The Heroes of Tomorrow Awards are a flagship initiative powered by the UN SDG Action Campaign, a special initiative of the UN Secretary-General hosted by UNDP.

The program aims to:

  • Recognize impactful changemakers across the globe
  • Provide visibility and amplification for transformative initiatives
  • Strengthen global collaboration around the SDGs
  • Inspire others to take action toward sustainable development

Each year, the program highlights individuals and organizations working—often against the odds—to drive progress in areas such as human rights, climate action, social justice, and community resilience.

What Makes This Award Unique?

The Heroes of Tomorrow Awards go beyond recognition—they provide a platform for growth, visibility, and global engagement.

Key Highlights:

  1. Global Recognition
    • Winners and finalists gain international visibility
    • Work is showcased to global audiences
  2. Capacity Building
    • Finalists receive coaching and training
    • Access to leadership and impact development sessions
  3. Networking Opportunities
    • Connect with global leaders, policymakers, and changemakers
  4. Global Broadcast
    • Awards ceremony is streamed on UN WebTV
    • Coverage by international media including RAI (Italy)

Awards Ceremony Details

  • Date: October 29, 2026
  • Location: Rome, Italy
  • Format: Live global event with international broadcast

Finalists will also participate in a dedicated program in Rome on October 28, 2026, leading up to the main ceremony.

Award Categories

The 2026 edition features three major categories recognizing diverse forms of impact:

1. Changemaker Award

This category honors individuals leading transformative action toward justice, equality, and peace.

Key Criteria:

  • Visionary leadership
  • Measurable and lasting impact
  • Community-driven initiatives

Application Type:

  • Individual nominations (including self-nomination)

2. Creativity Award

This category recognizes initiatives that use creativity to inspire change and action.

Key Criteria:

  • Innovative use of art, media, or storytelling
  • Ability to influence behavior or policy
  • Measurable impact through creative expression

Application Type:

  • Initiative-based applications

3. Resilience Award

This category celebrates initiatives building resilience in challenging environments.

Key Criteria:

  • Adaptability in the face of adversity
  • Solutions addressing climate, conflict, or inequality
  • Tangible and sustainable outcomes

Application Type:

  • Initiative-based applications

Program Timeline

Understanding the timeline is essential for applicants:

  1. January – June 2026
    • Formation of the High-Level Panel of Judges
  2. April 15 – May 17, 2026
    • Global call for applications
  3. May – August 2026
    • Internal review and due diligence
  4. September 2026
    • Notification of finalists
  5. September – October 2026
    • Public announcement of finalists
    • Judges’ review process
  6. October 2026
    • Announcement of judges
  7. October 28, 2026
    • Finalists’ program in Rome
  8. October 29, 2026
    • Awards ceremony

Who Should Apply?

The program is designed for:

  • Individuals driving social impact
  • Organizations and initiatives tackling global challenges
  • Creatives using art for advocacy
  • Leaders addressing climate change, inequality, and human rights

Past winners include individuals who have transformed communities through mental health support, gender equality advocacy, and peacebuilding efforts.

Why You Should Apply

The Heroes of Tomorrow Awards provide a unique opportunity to:

  • Gain global recognition for your work
  • Access capacity-building resources
  • Expand your international network
  • Inspire change beyond your immediate community

This is more than an award—it is a global platform for impact.

How to Apply

Applicants must:

  1. Select the appropriate award category
  2. Prepare a compelling application or nomination
  3. Submit before the deadline

Ensure your submission clearly demonstrates impact, innovation, and alignment with the SDGs.

APPLY HERE

Click HERE to learn more about the Heroes of Tomorrow: UN SDG Action Awards 2026.

Discover more global Award opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Heroes of Tomorrow: UN SDG Action Awards 2026 – Global Recognition & Impact Platform for Changemakers Driving Sustainable Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Eisenhower Fellowships 2027 Global Program: Fully Funded Leadership Fellowship in the USA for Emerging Global Leaders
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Eisenhower Fellowships 2027 Global Program is a prestigious and highly competitive international fellowship designed to bring together visionary leaders from around the world for an immersive leadership development experience in the United States. This nearly six-week program combines in-person and virtual engagement, enabling selected Fellows to design impactful projects, build global partnerships, and collaborate […]

The post Eisenhower Fellowships 2027 Global Program: Fully Funded Leadership Fellowship in the USA for Emerging Global Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Eisenhower Fellowships 2027 Global Program is a prestigious and highly competitive international fellowship designed to bring together visionary leaders from around the world for an immersive leadership development experience in the United States.

This nearly six-week program combines in-person and virtual engagement, enabling selected Fellows to design impactful projects, build global partnerships, and collaborate with American counterparts and a powerful global network of changemakers.

Application Deadline

The application window for the 2027 Global Program is:

April 13 – May 15, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to prepare early and submit strong, well-developed applications before the deadline.

About Eisenhower Fellowships

Eisenhower Fellowships is a global leadership initiative that identifies, empowers, and connects innovative leaders committed to creating a more peaceful, prosperous, and just world.

Through its flagship Global Program, the fellowship:

  • Brings together a diverse cohort of leaders from across continents
  • Promotes international understanding through dialogue and collaboration
  • Supports fellows in launching impactful, real-world projects
  • Provides access to a lifelong global leadership network

Participants are selected based on leadership track record, future potential, and commitment to long-term engagement with the fellowship community.

What the 2027 Global Program Offers

The fellowship provides a transformative experience that combines leadership development, collaboration, and global exposure.

Key Benefits

  1. Fully Immersive U.S. Experience
    • Nearly six weeks of in-person and virtual programming
    • Full-time participation required during the in-person phase
  2. Personalized Project Development
    • Fellows design and implement impactful projects
    • Focus on solving real-world challenges
  3. High-Level Networking
    • Engagement with American leaders and institutions
    • Collaboration with a diverse international cohort
  4. Access to a Prestigious Global Network
    • Join over 1,900 active fellows worldwide
    • Lifelong engagement with global changemakers
  5. Cross-Sector Collaboration
    • Work with professionals across industries
    • Build partnerships that extend beyond the program

Program Structure and Timeline

The fellowship is carefully structured to maximize impact and engagement:

1. Application Phase

  • April 13 – May 15, 2026

2. Review and Interviews

  • June – August 2026

3. Selection Announcement

  • September 2026

4. Fellowship Program (In-Person)

  • Early April – Mid-May 2027
  • Approximately five-and-a-half weeks in the United States

During this period, fellows will engage in intensive learning, networking, and project development activities.

What Fellows Will Do

Participants in the Global Program will:

  • Develop and refine a project addressing global or local challenges
  • Engage in professional exchanges with U.S. experts
  • Participate in leadership development sessions
  • Build meaningful collaborations with peers and mentors
  • Expand their global influence through strategic networking

The program emphasizes practical impact, ensuring that fellows leave with actionable solutions and partnerships.

Additional Eisenhower Fellowship Opportunities

While the 2027 Global Program is currently open, Eisenhower Fellowships also runs other programs:

1. Fall 2026 Innovative Entrepreneurs Program

  • Targets entrepreneurs aged 32–45
  • Focuses on for-profit ventures with social impact
  • Includes collaboration with U.S.-based fellows

2. 2026 USA Program

  • American fellows engage in both domestic and international travel
  • Work closely with international fellows

3. Eisenhower Global Scholars Program

  • Supports postgraduate study in Europe
  • Includes degrees from:
    • University of Oxford (UK)
    • IE University (Spain)

The Eisenhower Fellows Network

One of the most powerful aspects of the program is its global network:

  • Nearly 2,600 fellows
  • Representation from over 118 countries
  • Ongoing collaboration and lifelong engagement

Fellows commit to continued participation and contribution to the network, ensuring sustained global impact.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship seeks individuals who:

  • Demonstrate strong leadership and influence
  • Have a proven track record of impact
  • Show potential for future global contributions
  • Are committed to fostering international collaboration

Applicants should be ready to fully engage in a rigorous and immersive experience.

Why You Should Apply

The Eisenhower Fellowships Global Program is more than a leadership program—it is a platform for global transformation.

Key Reasons to Apply:

  • Gain international recognition
  • Expand your global network
  • Develop impactful solutions
  • Collaborate with world-class leaders
  • Become part of a lifelong leadership community

How to Apply

To apply:

  1. Review Eligibility and Selection Criteria
  2. Prepare your application materials
  3. Submit your application within the deadline

Click HERE to learn more and apply

For inquiries, applicants can contact:
apply@efworld.org

Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Eisenhower Fellowships 2027 Global Program: Fully Funded Leadership Fellowship in the USA for Emerging Global Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Entrepreneurship World Cup 2026: Win Up to $1M in Equity-Free Funding for Your Startup
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Entrepreneurship World Cup 2026 is now open for applications, offering startups worldwide the chance to compete for up to $1 million in equity-free funding. Designed for entrepreneurs at every stage—from idea to growth—this global competition provides access to mentorship, investor networks, and international exposure. Whether you are building your first prototype or scaling an […]

The post Entrepreneurship World Cup 2026: Win Up to $1M in Equity-Free Funding for Your Startup appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Entrepreneurship World Cup 2026 is now open for applications, offering startups worldwide the chance to compete for up to $1 million in equity-free funding. Designed for entrepreneurs at every stage—from idea to growth—this global competition provides access to mentorship, investor networks, and international exposure. Whether you are building your first prototype or scaling an established company, the programme offers a powerful platform to accelerate your startup journey.

About the Program

The Entrepreneurship World Cup (EWC) is one of the largest and most diverse global startup pitch competitions. It brings together entrepreneurs from across the world to showcase innovative solutions, connect with investors, and compete for significant funding opportunities.

The competition is structured across four main categories: idea-stage, early-stage, growth-stage, and AI-focused sub-tracks. This ensures that startups at different phases of development can participate and receive tailored support.

Applicants go through a multi-stage selection process, beginning with global applications. Selected startups progress through bootcamps, mentorship sessions, and pitch training before advancing to the Global Finals. The final stage takes place in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, where top startups present their ventures live to a global audience of investors, corporate partners, and industry leaders.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Access to funding and global networks remains one of the biggest challenges for startups, particularly in emerging markets. The Entrepreneurship World Cup addresses this gap by offering not only financial support but also strategic guidance and international visibility.

This competition is more than a pitch event. It is an ecosystem designed to help founders refine their business models, strengthen their market positioning, and connect with key stakeholders who can support long-term growth.

For entrepreneurs looking to expand into new markets, particularly in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region, the programme also provides valuable soft-landing support, making it easier to navigate new business environments.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in the Entrepreneurship World Cup gain access to a wide range of benefits that extend far beyond prize money.

The competition offers equity-free cash prizes totaling $1 million, allowing founders to scale their ventures without giving up ownership. In addition, the top 250 startups receive intensive mentorship, pitch training, and investor readiness support, helping them refine their strategies and improve their chances of securing further funding.

Startups that advance to the Global Finals gain unparalleled exposure by pitching on an international stage. This creates opportunities to connect with venture capitalists, corporate partners, and ecosystem leaders from around the world.

Participants also benefit from networking opportunities and market expansion support, particularly for those interested in entering the Saudi Arabian and broader MENA markets. These connections and resources can play a critical role in accelerating business growth and unlocking new opportunities.

Who Can Apply

The Entrepreneurship World Cup is open to startups at all stages of development.

Entrepreneurs at the idea stage can apply if they have a concept or early prototype and are looking to validate their idea. Early-stage startups with a product in the market and initial traction are also encouraged to participate. Growth-stage companies that are scaling operations or expanding into new markets can apply as well.

In addition, startups working on artificial intelligence solutions can compete in dedicated AI sub-tracks, which offer specialised prizes for innovation in areas such as infrastructure, sustainability, and emerging technologies.

Eligible Areas

The competition is open globally. Entrepreneurs from all countries are eligible to apply.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply, candidates must complete an online application through the official Entrepreneurship World Cup platform. The selection process includes multiple stages, starting with application review and followed by bootcamps, mentorship, and pitching rounds.

Applications are open until May 2026, and early submission is encouraged due to the competitive nature of the programme. Successful applicants will be notified as they progress through each stage of the competition.

Learn more about the program on official Entrepreneurship World Cup platform.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Entrepreneurship World Cup 2026: Win Up to $1M in Equity-Free Funding for Your Startup appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded UN Internship 2026–2027 (Open to All Countries + Remote Option) | UNU Junior Fellows Programme, Tokyo
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Are you a graduate student or early-career professional looking to gain hands-on experience within the United Nations system? The United Nations University (UNU) is offering a prestigious Junior Fellows Internship Programme at its headquarters in Tokyo, Japan—with flexibility for remote participation and applications open globally. Key Details (At a Glance) Programme: UNU Junior Fellows Internship […]

The post Fully Funded UN Internship 2026–2027 (Open to All Countries + Remote Option) | UNU Junior Fellows Programme, Tokyo appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a graduate student or early-career professional looking to gain hands-on experience within the United Nations system? The United Nations University (UNU) is offering a prestigious Junior Fellows Internship Programme at its headquarters in Tokyo, Japan—with flexibility for remote participation and applications open globally.

Key Details (At a Glance)

  • Programme: UNU Junior Fellows Internship
  • Location: Tokyo, Japan (On-site + some remote flexibility)
  • Eligibility: Open to applicants worldwide
  • Duration: ~6 months (24 weeks)
  • Start Date: 1 September 2026
  • End Date: 26 February 2027
  • Application Deadline: 8 May 2026
  • Working Hours: Full-time (Monday–Friday, 9:30–17:30)

About United Nations University

For the past four decades, UNU has been a go-to think tank for impartial research on the pressing global problems of human survival, conflict prevention, development, and welfare. With more than 400 researchers in 13 countries, UNU’s work spans the full breadth of the 17 Sustainable Development Goals, generating policy-relevant knowledge to effect positive global change. UNU maintains more than 200 collaborations with UN agencies and leading universities and research institutions across the globe.

For more information, please visit http://unu.edu.

 

About Junior Fellows Internship Programme in the Office of the Rector

The United Nations University Office of the Rector recruits highly qualified applicants to work as Junior Fellows at the UNU headquarters in Tokyo. Junior Fellows are recruited through a competitive application process twice per year. Graduate students interested in the work of the United Nations — and in particular, UNU — are encouraged to apply.

 

The underlying principles of the Junior Fellows Internship Programme at the Office of the Rector are centred on creating a dynamic, challenging and rewarding experience for graduate level students and young professionals. Successful candidates will contribute to the work of the Office of the Rector, the United Nations University, and the UN system as a whole. The programme provides opportunities for the development of new skills and knowledge and is a unique and constructive setting for the practical application of capacities acquired through graduate studies.

 

The programme places a strong emphasis on the training, guidance, and hands-on experience that form the foundation of a successful and beneficial internship. The programme opens with a one-week orientation designed to familiarise junior fellows with UNU, their co-workers, facilities and the working processes within the Office of the Rector. The knowledge and practical experience gained will also be beneficial to those candidates seeking a career in the United Nations. Junior fellows at the Office of the Rector can expect to gain valuable work experience while contributing in meaningful ways to UNU’s mission.

Purpose

The purpose of the Junior Fellows Internship Programme at the Office of the Rector is:

  • to promote interaction and dialogue between young scholars, professionals and UNU;
  • to familiarise junior fellows with the activities of the Office of the Rector and the work of UNU;
  • to provide opportunities for young professionals to gain in-depth, hands-on experience in a UN agency;
  • to expose young professionals to an administrative working environment, and;
  • to provide support to activities within the Office of the Rector.

Working Conditions

Running parallel to most university semesters, Junior Fellows are selected twice per year, once in May-June for the fall term and once in September-October for the spring term. The spring term runs from late February to late August, and the fall term runs from late August to late February. Work is primarily on-site at the UNU headquarters in Tokyo, with some flexibility for remote work.

Please note that for the Fall 2026 term, applicants located BOTH in Japan and overseas will be considered regardless of their current location.

 

Junior Fellows work full time during the regular working hours of the University: 9:30–17:30, Monday to Friday. Weekends and official UNU holidays are days off. In addition, Junior Fellows are entitled to 1.5 days of leave per month. When Junior Fellows work on-site, they are provided office space and facilities, as appropriate for their work. For remote work, Junior Fellows are expected to have access to a personal laptop or desktop with basic functionalities and internet for the duration of their internship. Junior Fellows receive a monthly stipend to help cover a portion of living costs, including transport to and from UNU headquarters located in Shibuya. Junior Fellows enjoy free on-site and online access to the headquarters’ library and gym (as available) and have the unique opportunity to learn about, contribute to and provide assistance in organising conferences, lectures and academic forums and symposiums (offline and online) that take place throughout the year.

Stipend

Interns who are not in receipt of financial support from other sources such as universities or other institutions, will receive a stipend from UNU to partially subsidise their basic living costs for the duration of the internship (note: scholarships for academic studies will not be considered a source of financial support for the purposes of the internship stipend).  The monthly amount of the stipend will be determined for each duty station based on the stipend rates in effect. The stipend will be paid on a monthly basis and part-time internship arrangements are prorated accordingly.

 

Responsibilities

 

Participants in this programme have the opportunity to engage in a number of ongoing initiatives that provide a unique window into the working processes of the University. Junior Fellows support the work of the Office of the Rector in the following areas:

 

  • administrative research and writing for institutional development: preparation of executive briefs on priority issues; background research and drafting of topical information briefs in support of internal policy development (i.e. related to the functioning of the University);
  • planning: supporting the development of project management tools and systems;
  • analysis: Use of analytical tools (including Microsoft Excel) to conduct internal analyses on university operations;
  • in person and online event coordination: supporting the organisation of lectures, conferences, workshops, and other public events;
  • meeting coordination: logistical support and minute taking;
  • editorial support: proof-reading reports and copy editing;
  • communication: preparation and dissemination of newsletters, event summaries, and other communication documents. 

Competencies

 

Values:

  • Inclusion – Take action to create an environment of dignity and respect for all, regardless of age, culture, disability, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, geography, grade, language, nationality, racial identity, religion, sex, sex characteristics, sexual orientation, social origin or any other aspect of identity.
  • Integrity – Act ethically, demonstrating the standards of conduct of the United Nations and taking prompt action in case of witnessing unprofessional or unethical behaviour, or any other breach of UN standards.
  • Humility – Demonstrate self-awareness and willingness to learn from others.
  • Humanity – Act according to the purposes of the United Nations: peace, dignity and equality on a healthy planet. 

Behaviours:

  • Connect and Collaborate – Build positive relationships with others to advance the work of the United Nations and work coherently as One UN
  • Analyse and Plan – Seek out and use data from a wide range of sources to understand problems, inform decision-making, propose evidence-based solutions and plan action
  • Deliver Results with Positive Impact – Hold oneself and others accountable for delivering results and making a positive difference to the people and causes that the United Nations serves
  • Learn and Develop – Pursue own learning and development and contribute to the learning and development of others
  • Adapt and Innovate – Demonstrate flexibility, agility and the ability to think and act in novel ways

You may wish to refer to the UN Values and Behaviours Framework for more information.

Qualifications

The qualifications that are considered in each application process include, but are not limited to, the following. The candidate:

  • is currently pursuing or has recently completed (within two years of application) postgraduate studies (master’s or doctoral), preferably in a field related to the University’s work;
  • is under 32 years of age at the time of application;
  • must not have more than 5 years of work experience in their related field;
  • has a native or fluent command of written and spoken English;
  • has excellent research, writing and analytical skills;
  • can establish and maintain effective working relations with people from diverse backgrounds;
  • demonstrates resourcefulness and creativity in problem-solving;
  • has advanced computer skills: sound, hands-on, knowledge of MS Office applications (familiarity with ICT tools and high level of proficiency in Microsoft Excel are desirable);
  • has a demonstrated interest in the work of the United Nations and more specifically, in the work of the United Nations University. 

Duration of contract: The duration of the internship is approximately 6 months (24 weeks).

Expected start date: 1st September 2026

Expected end date: 26th February 2027

 

How to Apply:

To apply to UNU, you will not need an account. Instead, we ask that you:

  • Application: Apply via the apply link.
  • Answer a few questions that are tailored to the position.
  • CV: Attach a current and complete CV in English (in the place where it says to upload the P11 Form). Your résumé or c.v. may not exceed two single-sided pages. The P11 Form is not necessary for this position, please upload your current CV.
  • Cover Letter: Insert your Cover Letter (in English) as required, stating interest in and qualifications for the post. Your cover letter should not be longer than one single-sided page and should include the following: i. your motivations for applying to the programme; ii. how you feel you may contribute to the work being done at the Office of the Rector; iii. how this programme relates to your future career goals.
  • Application form: Fill in the Application Form and upload via the link. The form may be completed using MS Word or printed and filled in by hand.
  • Funding form: Fill in the Funding Form and upload via the link. The form should outline the source, amount, and duration of actual and/or projected funding while in Tokyo.
  • Letters of Recommendation:
    • Arrange for two letters of recommendation from referees who are familiar with your character and who have agreed to write a recommendation on your behalf. UNU reserves the right to contact your referees. Certain candidates may have difficulty obtaining academic references and it may be more appropriate to get references from professionals or supervisors. Of the two referees issuing letters of recommendation, it is expected that at least one is a professor acquainted with your academic abilities. Referees should not be family members or friends. The two referees will need to each complete a letter of recommendation.
    • The letter of recommendation must be submitted by the referee via email to the following address: ro_internship@unu.edu. Recommendation letters submitted by the applicant themselves will not be considered. Referees must use their official (institutional/academic/corporate) email address to submit the letter.
  • Language Certification: Applicants must provide certification in English if they do not meet either of the two following criteria:
    i. the applicant’s native language is English, or
    ii. the applicant completed an undergraduate programme or is enrolled in a graduate program with English as the medium of instruction.
    In the case that the applicant does not meet either of the above criteria, a valid TOEFL, IELTS or TOEIC score report is required. The original certificate or photocopy is accepted. Preferred score ranges for each test are:

 

Application Deadline:  8th May 2026 (NOTE: applications will NOT be possible after 23:59 Japan Standard Time)

 

Assessment

Application Process

The application process is conducted in three stages. Instructions for each stage are detailed below. Any application that does not comply with the information provided on this page will not be considered.

 

Stage 1

The first stage of the application process is designed to measure the applicant’s motivation and background and assess how the applicant’s qualifications meet the criteria for selection. Stage 1 requires submission of the documents listed above. Evaluation of qualified candidates may include a desk review and/or a competency-based interview, and references.

 

Stage 2

Applicants who are shortlisted proceed to the second stage which consists of a telephone or Zoom interview that will be arranged in advance with each shortlisted applicant. The purpose of the interview is to help the Internship Committee better understand the applicant’s motivations, expectations, and goals vis-à-vis participation in the Junior Fellows Internship Programme at the Office of the Rector. The interview will also assess the applicant’s communication skills, interpersonal skills and proficiency in English. Further details and an appointment schedule will be provided at the closing of Stage 1. Please note that only shortlisted candidates may be contacted.

Stage 3

The applicants will be asked to complete a short time-restricted assignment similar to the tasks they would be working on during the internship. This assignment will be conducted on the same day as the interview at an agreed time. Candidates should allow up to 2 hours to complete the assignment.

APPLY NOW 

Acceptance

Successful applicants will be required to sign an Agreement Form that will act as the working contract between the junior fellow and the Office of the Rector for the duration of the internship. This form will be mailed to those selected to join the programme. A signed and dated form must be returned to the Office of the Rector in order for the application to be complete. Once completed, the selected applicants will begin their application for a Japanese visa, if necessary.

Please note that for the Fall 2026 term, applicants located BOTH in Japan and overseas will be considered.


Special notice
The purpose of the Internship Programme is not to lead to further employment with UNU but to complement an intern’s studies. Therefore, there should be no expectation of employment at the end of an internship.

Interns do not hold international civil servant status nor are they considered a “staff member” as defined in the United Nations Staff Rules and Regulations and may not represent UNU in any official capacity.

UNU accepts no responsibility for costs arising from accidents and/or illness or death incurred during the internship.
UNU is committed to diversity and inclusion within its workforce, and encourages all candidates, irrespective of gender, nationality, religious and ethnic backgrounds, including persons living with disabilities to apply. Applications from developing countries, and from women are strongly encouraged.

UNU has a zero-tolerance policy on conduct that is incompatible with the aims and objectives of the United Nations and UNU, including sexual exploitation and abuse, sexual harassment, abuse of authority and discrimination.

Information about UNU rosters

UNU reserves the right to select one or more candidates from this internship announcement. We may also retain applications and consider candidates applying to this internship cohort for upcoming internships within the next 2 years.

Scam warning
UNU does not charge any application, processing, training, interviewing, testing or other fee in connection with the application or recruitment process. Should you receive a solicitation for the payment of a fee, please disregard it. Furthermore, please note that emblems, logos, names and addresses are easily copied and reproduced. Therefore, you are advised to apply particular care when submitting personal information on the web.

APPLY NOW 
FOR MORE INTERNSHIPS PLEASE CLICK HERE
FOR MORE UNITED NATIONS OPPORTUNITIES PLEASE CLICK HERE 

The post Fully Funded UN Internship 2026–2027 (Open to All Countries + Remote Option) | UNU Junior Fellows Programme, Tokyo appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers in Central & Eastern Europe
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Apply for the Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026 and Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town Young leaders across Central and Eastern Europe now have the chance to access one of the most prestigious global leadership opportunities through the Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026. This fully funded scholarship will support 13 outstanding young […]

The post Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers in Central & Eastern Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Apply for the Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026 and Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town

Young leaders across Central and Eastern Europe now have the chance to access one of the most prestigious global leadership opportunities through the Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026. This fully funded scholarship will support 13 outstanding young changemakers to attend the One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town, South Africa, from 3–6 November 2026.

If you are passionate about transforming the future of finance, business, sustainability, technology, or economic development, this opportunity could be the next major step in your leadership journey.

What is the Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026?

The Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship was created to empower talented youth in Central and Eastern Europe who are shaping solutions to today’s financial and societal challenges. As economies evolve rapidly, many communities still face barriers such as financial exclusion, limited literacy, and unequal access to opportunities.

This scholarship seeks to equip future leaders with global exposure, mentorship, and international networks so they can return home and create lasting impact.

Through this initiative, selected scholars will join the global One Young World Community, a network of more than 22,000 young leaders from over 190 countries.

Scholarship Benefits

Successful applicants will receive:

  • A fully funded place at the One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town
  • Access to a global network of young leaders and professionals
  • Lifetime membership in the One Young World community
  • Leadership development opportunities
  • Structured mentorship programs such as the Action Accelerator
  • International exposure and collaboration opportunities
  • Skills, insights, and connections to drive change in local communities

Who Should Apply?

This scholarship is ideal for ambitious young people working or studying in areas such as:

  • Finance
  • Business Analytics
  • Economics
  • Mathematics
  • Statistics
  • Technology
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Public Policy
  • Research
  • Community Development

Applicants should also have a strong interest in solving challenges related to:

  • Financial inclusion
  • Sustainable finance
  • Economic resilience
  • Societal development
  • Long-term value creation

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify, applicants must:

  • Be aged 18 to 30 at the time of the Summit
  • Be from or actively contributing in eligible Central European countries
  • Demonstrate leadership potential and community impact
  • Show strong academic achievement or professional excellence
  • Possess analytical skills, innovation, and intellectual curiosity
  • Be committed to creating positive change in society
  • Submit truthful and non-discriminatory applications

Applicants over 30 may still be considered if they demonstrate exceptional impact and leadership.

Eligible Countries

Applications are open to candidates from:

  • Austria
  • Bulgaria
  • Croatia
  • Czech Republic
  • Hungary
  • Poland
  • Romania
  • Slovakia

Why You Should Apply

The Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship is more than financial support. It is a gateway to global leadership, lifelong mentorship, and the opportunity to collaborate with changemakers from around the world.

For young leaders in Central and Eastern Europe, this is a rare chance to gain visibility, strengthen your voice, and help shape the future of finance and society.

Application Deadline

  • Opening Date: 7 April 2026
  • Deadline: 18 May 2026

Early application is strongly recommended.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official One Young World scholarship portal.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more scholarship opportunities, click here. 

Final Thoughts

If you are building solutions in finance, business, technology, or sustainability and want to expand your impact globally, the Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026 is an opportunity you should not miss.

This could be your chance to represent your country, connect with world leaders, and become part of a movement creating a better future.

The post Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers in Central & Eastern Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: FCI4Africa Open Call 2026: Get Up to €50,000 Funding for Food System Innovation Projects in Africa
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Innovators, researchers, start-ups, and technology-driven organizations now have a major opportunity to secure funding for transformative food system solutions in Africa. The FCI4Africa Open Call 1 (OC1) has officially launched, offering up to €50,000 per project to selected applicants working on innovative ideas that strengthen fairness, resilience, and sustainability across African food value chains. Backed […]

The post FCI4Africa Open Call 2026: Get Up to €50,000 Funding for Food System Innovation Projects in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Innovators, researchers, start-ups, and technology-driven organizations now have a major opportunity to secure funding for transformative food system solutions in Africa. The FCI4Africa Open Call 1 (OC1) has officially launched, offering up to €50,000 per project to selected applicants working on innovative ideas that strengthen fairness, resilience, and sustainability across African food value chains.

Backed by the Horizon Europe programme, FCI4Africa is focused on building more inclusive food systems by supporting practical innovations, research-driven tools, and scalable technologies that solve key challenges across the continent.

With a total funding pool of €400,000, eight selected sub-projects will receive financial support to test, validate, and scale impactful solutions.

What is FCI4Africa Open Call 1?

FCI4Africa is a four-year international initiative running from 2024 to 2028 with 14 partners across 7 countries. Its mission is to improve food systems in Africa through innovation, fairness, and resilience.

The first Open Call is designed to attract forward-thinking applicants who can help solve food system challenges through technology, research, and new business models.

Applicants are invited to submit projects aligned with one of the following objectives:

Objective 1: Improve Existing FCI4Africa Tools and Concepts

Applicants can test, validate, and enhance existing business concepts, digital tools, and project use cases already developed within FCI4Africa.

Objective 2: Develop New Solutions for Specific Challenges

Applicants may also propose fresh ideas, tools, or technologies that directly address identified food system challenges under the FCI4Africa framework.

All selected projects are expected to generate useful datasets that can further improve project tools, methods, and business models.

Funding Details

  • Maximum funding per project: €50,000
  • Total call budget: €400,000
  • Number of projects to be funded: 8
  • Funding source: Horizon Europe

This funding can help innovators pilot technologies, validate business ideas, gather evidence, and demonstrate measurable impact.

Project Timeline

The selected projects will run from November 2026 to October 2027 through a structured three-phase implementation process:

Phase 1 – Design

November 2026 – January 2027
Duration: 3 Months
Deliverable: Activity Plan

Phase 2 – Development

February 2027 – July 2027
Duration: 6 Months
Deliverable: Results Demonstration

Phase 3 – Validation

August 2027 – October 2027
Duration: 3 Months
Deliverable: Dissemination Report and Lessons Learned

Who Can Apply?

Applications are open to individual organizations or entities only. Consortia are not allowed.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Universities
  • Research institutes
  • Technology providers
  • R&D SMEs
  • Innovation start-ups
  • Industry actors building new technologies
  • Multidisciplinary stakeholders

Why You Should Apply

This opportunity offers more than funding. It provides a pathway to test solutions in real-world markets, contribute to Africa’s development, and collaborate with a leading international innovation network.

Key Benefits

  • Access up to €50,000 in project funding
  • Gain recognition through a Horizon Europe-backed programme
  • Validate your innovation in practical environments
  • Contribute to sustainable food systems in Africa
  • Build datasets and evidence for future scaling
  • Connect with global experts and innovation partners

How to Apply

Applicants must submit proposals online through the official Open Call platform.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more grant opportunities, click here. 

Deadline

June 30, 2026 at 17:00 CEST (Brussels Time)

Late applications will not be considered, so early submission is strongly advised.

Final Thoughts

The FCI4Africa Open Call 2026 is a high-value opportunity for innovators committed to solving Africa’s food system challenges. Whether you are a start-up building agri-tech solutions, a university conducting research, or an SME creating sustainable tools, this grant can help bring your ideas to life.

If your project can improve fairness, resilience, and inclusivity in food value chains, now is the time to apply.

The post FCI4Africa Open Call 2026: Get Up to €50,000 Funding for Food System Innovation Projects in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Your Green Action Fellowship 2026: Fully Online Climate Leadership Program for Young Changemakers Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Young people around the world are increasingly concerned about climate change, sustainability, and the future of the planet. However, many struggle to find practical ways to make a real difference in their daily lives. The Your Green Action Fellowship 2026 was created to solve that challenge by helping youth transform climate awareness into meaningful action. […]

The post Your Green Action Fellowship 2026: Fully Online Climate Leadership Program for Young Changemakers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young people around the world are increasingly concerned about climate change, sustainability, and the future of the planet. However, many struggle to find practical ways to make a real difference in their daily lives. The Your Green Action Fellowship 2026 was created to solve that challenge by helping youth transform climate awareness into meaningful action.

This fully online environmental fellowship is designed for young people who want to learn, grow, and take practical steps toward sustainability. Through engaging lessons, weekly activities, and a supportive global community, participants will gain the tools to create positive change in their homes, schools, workplaces, and communities.

If you are passionate about the environment and want to move from concern to action, this fellowship is the perfect opportunity.

What is the Your Green Action Fellowship?

The Your Green Action Fellowship is a structured online program focused on practical climate action. Rather than teaching theory alone, the fellowship helps participants understand how their everyday choices affect the environment and how small actions can create lasting impact.

Participants will explore key issues such as food systems, waste, energy use, transportation, climate justice, sustainable consumption, and advocacy.

The program also encourages reflection, habit-building, leadership, and storytelling to help fellows inspire change in others.

Program Benefits

Participants selected for the fellowship will receive:

  • Access to short and practical learning sessions
  • Weekly activities focused on real-life climate action
  • Educational resources and curated materials
  • Community experience with like-minded young people worldwide
  • Leadership and advocacy development opportunities
  • A personal Green Action Plan for long-term impact
  • Certificate of Completion after successfully finishing the program
  • Flexible self-paced learning format

What You Will Learn

During the fellowship, participants will gain knowledge and practical skills in the following areas:

  • Climate change and its connection to everyday habits
  • Environmental impact of food, fashion, waste, and energy
  • Sustainable habits that are realistic and accessible
  • Climate justice and global inequalities
  • Reducing your personal environmental footprint
  • Influencing others through positive leadership
  • Storytelling for environmental advocacy
  • Creating long-term sustainability goals

Fellowship Journey

The program covers a step-by-step learning journey, including:

  • Climate Change & Your Role
  • Water Conservation
  • Sustainable Food Choices
  • Ethical Fashion
  • Energy Awareness
  • Waste Reduction
  • Technology & Digital Sustainability
  • Transportation & Mobility
  • Conscious Consumption
  • Community Action & Leadership
  • Climate Storytelling & Advocacy
  • Personal Green Action Plan & Graduation

Why This Fellowship Stands Out

Many climate programs focus heavily on information but provide little guidance for real-life implementation. The Your Green Action Fellowship stands out because it is action-oriented.

Participants do not just learn about climate problems. They build sustainable habits, join a global network, and gain confidence to lead by example.

This combination of education, action, and community makes the fellowship ideal for youth who want practical environmental impact.

Who Should Apply?

This fellowship is ideal for:

  • Students
  • Young professionals
  • Youth leaders
  • Climate beginners
  • Sustainability advocates
  • Community changemakers

No prior environmental knowledge is required.

Global Perspective

The fellowship recognizes that climate change affects regions differently, especially communities in the Global South that often face the greatest challenges despite contributing the least to emissions.

Participants will gain a broader understanding of global inequalities while connecting environmental issues to their local realities.

About the Organizers

Your Green Action is an initiative of the Wang and Tangang Foundation, an international NGO committed to advancing the Sustainable Development Goals through youth empowerment, environmental responsibility, and global citizenship.

The organization’s vision is reflected in its slogan:

Inspiring Action, Impacting Generations.

Application Deadline

The official application deadline is 26 April 2026. Applicants are encouraged to apply early.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the fellowship application carefully. Selected participants will be contacted via email.

Apply now and begin your journey toward a greener future.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

The post Your Green Action Fellowship 2026: Fully Online Climate Leadership Program for Young Changemakers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Citi Freshman Discovery Program 2026 (Virtual – USA): Early Career Opportunity for First-Year Students in Financial Services
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Are you a first-year university student looking to explore a future in finance, banking, or global business? The Citi Freshman Discovery Program 2026 is a highly valuable early-career opportunity designed to introduce students to the world of financial services through interactive workshops, networking sessions, and mentorship. This virtual program provides a structured platform for first-year […]

The post Citi Freshman Discovery Program 2026 (Virtual – USA): Early Career Opportunity for First-Year Students in Financial Services appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a first-year university student looking to explore a future in finance, banking, or global business? The Citi Freshman Discovery Program 2026 is a highly valuable early-career opportunity designed to introduce students to the world of financial services through interactive workshops, networking sessions, and mentorship.

This virtual program provides a structured platform for first-year students to gain exposure, build foundational knowledge, and begin shaping their professional journey with one of the world’s leading financial institutions.

Application Deadline

All applications must be submitted by:

April 27, 2026 (9:00 AM EST)

Late submissions will not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to apply early.

Program Dates and Format

  • Program Dates: May 19 – May 21, 2026
  • Format: Virtual
  • Time: Begins at 10:00 AM EST

The program is fully online, making it accessible to eligible students across the United States.

Overview of the Citi Freshman Discovery Program

The Citi Freshman Discovery Program is a virtual workshop series designed to help first-year college students:

  • Explore career opportunities in financial services
  • Gain technical and professional knowledge
  • Build networks with industry professionals
  • Develop clarity on career pathways early in their academic journey

Participants will engage directly with Citi’s Early Career team, gaining insight into real-world financial careers.

What Participants Will Experience

Selected students will take part in a dynamic and interactive learning experience.

Key Program Components:

  1. Virtual Workshops
    • Learn foundational concepts in financial services
    • Explore various career paths within Citi
  2. Networking Opportunities
    • Connect with professionals across Citi
    • Engage with peers and like-minded students
  3. Leadership Engagement
    • Participate in sessions with senior leaders
    • Gain insights into industry trends and expectations
  4. Career Development Sessions
    • Learn about early career planning
    • Develop professional and personal growth strategies

Program Benefits

The Citi Freshman Discovery Program offers a strong foundation for students at the beginning of their careers.

Key Benefits Include:

  1. Early Career Exposure
    • Understand the financial services industry at an early stage
  2. Professional Networking
    • Build relationships with industry experts and peers
  3. Skill Development
    • Gain technical knowledge and soft skills
  4. Mentorship Opportunities
    • Learn directly from Citi professionals
  5. Career Clarity
    • Discover potential career paths and interests

Eligibility Criteria

Academic Requirements:

Applicants must:

  • Be a first-year student currently enrolled in:
    • A four-year Bachelor’s degree program at an accredited US institution
      OR
    • A community college with plans to transfer to a four-year institution

Graduation Timeline:

  • Expected graduation between Fall 2028 and Spring 2029

Ideal Candidate Profile

Citi is looking for students who demonstrate:

  • Strong interest in financial services
  • Curiosity and willingness to learn
  • Teamwork and collaboration skills
  • Initiative and motivation for personal growth

Importantly, the program is open to students from all academic disciplines, including those with limited or no prior exposure to finance.

Selection Criteria

Applicants will be evaluated based on:

  • Academic performance
  • Leadership potential
  • Extracurricular involvement
  • Demonstrated interest in career development

A strong, well-prepared resume is required as part of the application.

Why You Should Apply

The Citi Freshman Discovery Program is a strategic stepping stone for students aiming to build careers in finance, banking, consulting, or related fields.

What Makes This Opportunity Valuable:

  • Provides early exposure to a competitive industry
  • Offers access to global professionals and mentors
  • Enhances your resume with a reputable brand
  • Helps you make informed career decisions early

How to Apply

To apply:

  1. Prepare an updated resume
  2. Ensure you meet all eligibility requirements
  3. Submit your application before the deadline

Applicants are encouraged to highlight leadership experience, curiosity, and interest in financial services.

APPLY HERE

Click HERE to learn more about the Citi Freshman Discovery Program 2026.

For more global Conference opportunities, visit Opportunities for Youth HERE.

The post Citi Freshman Discovery Program 2026 (Virtual – USA): Early Career Opportunity for First-Year Students in Financial Services appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
